key_insight: The paper introduces a monolithic finite element contact formulation that couples deformation, temperature, and interfacial bonding/debonding through a unified chemo-mechano-thermodynamic framework.
engineering_relevance: It enables simulation of complex adhesive contact processes such as pressure- or gap-dependent bonding, exothermic reactions, and thermal hardening/expansion in large-deformation solids.
method: Uses the contact theory of Sauer et al. (2022) with six coupled fields: two bodies’ deformations and temperatures plus interfacial bonding and interfacial temperature.
Implements bonding/debonding via elementary models built from a quadratic contact potential.
Provides full linearization for Newton-Raphson solution and supports classical or isogeometric shape functions with implicit time integration.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several challenging example problems demonstrating bonding, debonding, thermal effects, and simultaneous coupled behaviors, but does not give quantitative benchmark metrics.
watch_item: The formulation is computationally demanding and requires careful consistent linearization and robust handling of interface state evolution for stable Newton convergence.
paper_title: A coupled finite element formulation for chemo-mechano-thermodynamical contact and its application to bonding and debonding
